subject: [PATCH v2 12/21] fuse: add struct fuse_entry2_out and helpers for extended entry replies

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein questioned whether a compound response for LOOKUP+STATX would be beneficial, suggesting it could simplify code and improve handling of backing_id. He also mentioned a test patch on his fuse-backing-inode-wip branch that demonstrates setting backing inode passthrough on getattr.
reviews.raw_body:
I think you had a discussion with Miklos about whether
it makes sense to do a compound response for LOOKUP+STATX.
I don't remember the conclusion, but if this can save us all the
if (use_entry2) { } else {} all over the code, then I think it is worth it.

Can you give me the bottom line from your discussion or was there
no conclusion reached?

If we do that, where do we return backing_id?
Could make use of the reserved[] fields in fuse_statx_out.

I happen to have a test patch at the tip of my fuse-backing-inode-wip
branch:
6e296625eafd6 fuse: support setting backing inode passthrough on getattr

It's not using the statx response, and it was just done for testing,
but in theory,
if a server wants to  change an inode from non-passthrough to
yes-passthrough mid lifetime
doing this on attribute cache expiry may make more sense than on entry
timeout expiry, because backing_id is an attribute of the inode not the dentry.

Not sure if this is making any sense.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary: Reviewer Horst Birthelmer expressed uncertainty and lack of clarity regarding the use of compounds in FUSE, specifically whether they will be limited to fusex or also applied to classic FUSE, and apologized for not having a tested patch ready.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Amir,

I see there is a lot of talk about using the compounds in a couple of different contexts.
I really have to apologize for not having the latest patch ready by now, but I'm 
a bit unsure how to do it.

I'm not entirely sure if compounds will only be a thing for fusex or if it will ever
be accepted for the classic FUSE.

I actually have a patch that I have not tested well enough, since I have to maintain
our own version and then have to do the fusex one and probably the new verison for 
the classic FUSE version.

So, sorry about that, but I'm not having enough clarity on what to focus on.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a new extended entry reply structure called fuse_entry2_out, which carries additional information such as backing_id and statx attributes. It also introduces helpers to process this new structure for passthrough support in lookup, revalidate, and create operations. The existing fuse_statx_to_attr function is moved earlier in the file to avoid forward declarations.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledges a concern about how to handle fuse and fusex in the patch, but expresses uncertainty about Miklos' thoughts on the matter.

Author agrees that supporting yes passthrough -> no passthrough would require an ioctl mechanism and proposes consistent interfaces for both directions, but acknowledges the need for branching logic to handle new slimmed-down-lookup differently from old lookup.

Author expressed uncertainty about a future plan, indicating that further discussion or clarification is needed to resolve the issue.
reviews.raw_body:
I think the conclusion was that for fusex it will be compounded, but
I'm not sure what Miklos is thinking for fuse.

---

I like this idea but I think for supporting yes passthrough -> no
passthrough, it would have to go through an ioctl mechanism since the
getattr could be passed through. In that case, I was thinking it would
be better to have the interfaces be consistent (eg both yes
passthrough -> no passthrough and no passthrough -> yes passthrough go
through an ioctl interface).

I think even with the backing id as part of statx and lookup+statx
compounded together, there would still have to be the "if (use_entry2)
{} else {}" branching logic to handle the new slimmed-down-lookup
differently from old lookup).

---

I'm not sure what the plan is either. I think this will get clarified
on Thursday hopefully.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-05-18.patch_summary: This patch adds a new extended entry reply structure called fuse_entry2_out, which carries additional information such as backing_id and statx attributes. It also introduces helpers to process this new structure for passthrough support in lookup, revalidate, and create operations. The existing fuse_statx_to_attr function is moved earlier in the file to avoid forward declarations.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed confusion about whether the patch introduces a new FUSE call, indicating he was not aware of it and jokingly mentioned not receiving an invitation.
reviews.raw_body:
Wait, is there a FUSE call? I did not get an invite :-D

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Luis Henriques
reviews.summary:
reviewer pointed out that fuse_lookup_init() function does not handle the case where both fuse_entry_out and fuse_entry2_out are used simultaneously, which could lead to a memory leak due to double memset

reviewer expressed confusion about the handling of compound and file handles in fusex, citing a discrepancy between the original plan and the current implementation

Reviewer Luis Henriques made a lighthearted comment about not receiving an invitation to something, and did not provide any technical feedback on the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
(Sorry, I'm slow catching-up with email :-) )

On Tue, May 19 2026, Amir Goldstein wrote:

---

thought the plan was clear: both compound and file handles are going to be
in fusex.  Quoting:

---

Don't worry, I think no one has yet received an invite ;-)

Cheers,
-- 
Luís

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledges that there's uncertainty about whether the new struct fuse_entry2_out and helpers should be added to both fuse and fusex, but no clear plan is stated.

Author responded to a question about scheduling a meeting, stating that only the time was agreed upon and logistics are still being set up by John.
reviews.raw_body:
I think the part we're unclear about is whether the plan is to also
have this in fuse or just fusex.

---

I haven't either. I think the only thing we nailed down was the time
(Thursday at 20:30 CEDT) but I think John is still setting up the
logistics for it.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-05-19.patch_summary: This patch adds a new extended entry reply structure called fuse_entry2_out, which carries additional information such as backing_id and statx attributes. It also introduces helpers to process this new structure for passthrough support in lookup, revalidate, and create operations. The existing fuse_statx_to_attr function is moved earlier in the file to avoid forward declarations.
